comment.body:
Here is one more summary on why Alma's GEX varies from OD. Here are the key reasons cited for these discrepancies:
•
Differences in Calculation Methods and Weights: Alma states that she does not know exactly how OD calculates the exposure6. She suggests that her own model might include factors that OD's does not, such as a net zomma snapshot6. She also mentions the possibility of different weights being used in the calculations67.
•
Handling of Different Expirations Intraday: Alma and another member observed that the pre-market OD charts sometimes seem more accurate than the updated intraday ones8. Alma speculates that this might be because OD doesn't effectively differentiate the weights of different expirations as the day progresses, suggesting a vega model is needed for that8. Her own coded 3D gamma projection dynamically changes over time and across strikes, incorporating different tenors and their impacts59.
•
Specific Greek Calculations: Alma specifically noted a discrepancy in charm predictions between her model and OD's, suggesting OD's charm calculations might not be as accurate45. She mentions that volatility profile influences charm exposure on different strikes10.

comment.body: Wednesday ruling harmed the government’s diplomacy and intruded upon President Donald Trump’s exclusive authority to conduct foreign affairs, the Department of Justice said in a Thursday court filing. It asked the Federal Circuit to put the decision on hold while the administration pursued a formal appeal. “Absent at least interim relief from this court, the United States plans to seek emergency relief from the Supreme Court tomorrow,” the administration said.

comment.body: They did that to cut off the immediate right to appeal to USSC under the rule created by ACLU decision . . . now it will be decided at appellate level first.  The fact that markets didn't react -- we were already back to yesterday's levels before the ruling came out after hours anyway -- is telling.

comment.body: Yes, that's correct.  The ITC entered an injunction (order) prohibiting Trump admin from moving forward with any action implementing or enforcing certain of the tariffs.  The appellate court today, in an extraordinarily fast decision, "stayed" that injunction, meaning put it on hold while the appellate court considers the legal issues.  The plaintiff has until June 5 to file its opposition and then Trump admin until June 9 to reply.  Then maybe the appellate court rules within a week or so of that.  Then the loser will seek expedited review from Supreme Court.

Then the loser will seek expedited review from Supreme Court.
